title: Chalk whites the night fill disagrees with
oneline: Offline scorer works; ranking is near-all true positives, a dozen new flags
summary: Built the proposed scorer fully offline (zero Gemini calls): chalk-whitened regions (chalk-gate definitions, 2px-eroded) sampled against the committed night raw, scored by area x colored-fraction (chroma >= 40). 70 pages scored in ~50s; 33 score 0 (true agreements), and every eyeballed high scorer is a real disagreement. dog-tall collar and cat-tall ears confirmed as predicted; unicorn-tall's night fill actually COMPLIED with the chalk (score 0), but a one-line light-raw variant proves canon wants the horn/cheek tinted — closing that blind spot. New finds nobody listed: astronaut-wide's whited-out child face, two windshields, duck bill, pig snout, cow bell + muzzle, magenta web/umbrella flowers, teddy paw pad.
tried: Whitened-region detection reusing idea #2's inventory (chalk-gate INK/SLACK/flood definitions)
2px region erosion + chroma sampling of the committed night raw in the same 512 fit:fill space
Page ranking by area x coloredFrac, plus a chroma-weighted severity alternative
3-up evidence crops (chalk | night raw | shipped composite) for the top 20, eyeballed all
Light-raw variant (one-line change) as an independent 'what does canon want' signal
worked: Ranking is essentially all true positives; the 33 zero-score pages are genuine chalk/fill agreements
farm/dog-tall collar (chroma 136, canon red per light raw) and farm/cat-tall ears confirmed exactly as IDEAS.md predicted
Surfaced ~12 unlisted disagreements: astronaut-wide face, fire-tall/police-wide windshields, duck bill, pig snout, cow bell/muzzle, spider-wide + umbrella-wide flowers, teddy paw pad, dog-wide ear
Erode+MIN_SAMPLED eliminated misregistration false positives outright
Light-raw variant resolves night-compliant cases: unicorn horn is canon-tinted (chroma 43) even though the night fill painted it white
failed: IDEAS.md's unicorn-tall hypothesis is wrong on the night axis - the night fill agreed with the chalk (chroma 4-6), so the night-raw signal alone misses canon-vs-chalk disagreement when the fill complied
limitations: Finds disagreement, not wrongness - white celestial motifs at night are often deliberate; the list needs a human pass by design
CHROMA_MIN=40 misses pale pastels (cat nose at meanChroma 36 scored 0); lower to ~30 or report the 15-40 band
Light-raw signal over-flags at night (canon-yellow stars are deliberately white at night) - use only to adjudicate night-compliant regions
Region granularity is the chalk's: mixed regions (owl sclera + facial disc) dilute coloredFrac
